# RC-04: Decision Log

**Track:** Risk Controls (`RC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

Capture all significant implementation decisions — especially those made autonomously by agents — including the decision taken, rationale, alternatives considered, and the human who reviewed the decision. Provides the audit trail required by DORA and the AI Act.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent logs, human reviews |
| **Agent does** | Automatically log all autonomous decisions with rationale and alternatives considered. |
| **Human does** | Periodically review decision log; flag decisions that should have been escalated. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Implementation decision log | log_entry | Structured log of decisions made during implementation with rationale, alternatives, and reviewer attribution. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | log_and_continue |
| **Escalate to** | Tech Lead |

Decisions that cannot be logged are escalated to Tech Lead for manual recording.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 8(6) | Documentation of ICT risk management decisions throughout the change process |
| EU AI Act | Art. 12(1) | Automatic recording of events including agent decisions for high-risk AI systems |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Engineering · **Owner role:** Tech Lead
